message:
fcp-conformance: add spec-derived determinism matrix for canonical ids and zone ops

crates/fcp-conformance/src/vectors/core.rs grows a suite of
narrow, metamorphic-style tests that lock down the normative MUST
clauses from FCP V3 §6 around canonical serialization and keyed
ObjectId derivation. Each test targets one invariant so a regression
points at the exact violated rule:

  - ObjectId determinism:
      * same (body, zone, schema, key) produces the same id
      * call order (A-then-B vs B-then-A) is irrelevant, pinning the
        absence of hidden global state in the derivation path
      * changing any of the four inputs changes the id — one test per
        input axis: body, zone, schema, key
  - Canonical serialization:
      * round-trip via CanonicalSerializer recovers the input struct
      * two independent serializations of the same value yield byte-
        equal output
  - ZoneId:
      * hash output is 32 bytes (the BLAKE3 size the spec expects)
      * all five canonical zones hash to distinct values
      * from_tailscale_tag rejects strings without the `tag:fcp-`
        prefix, covering empty, hyphenless, non-fcp-prefixed, and
        bare-word inputs

These tests are pure additions in the existing `mod tests` block; no
production code changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
